headlines.title: Rescue operations under way after US refuelling plane crashes in Iraq
headlines.description: The US Central Command says rescue efforts are under way after a refuelling aircraft went down over western Iraq.

headlines.title: UK economy saw zero growth in January ahead of Iran war
headlines.description: Analysts had been expecting 0.2% growth for the UK economy at the beginning of the year.

headlines.title: Winners, Sinners and record breakers: 17 fun facts about this year's Oscars
headlines.description: Sinners, Marty Supreme, Hamnet and One Battle After Another are among the films in contention this year.

headlines.title: Oil price profiteering will not be tolerated, says Miliband
headlines.description: Ed Miliband says the competition watchdog is primed to intervene if firms use the oil price shock to "rip off" customers.

headlines.title: How long will the cold weather last?
headlines.description: Friday feels noticeably colder. Helen Willetts explains why there's been a change in temperature and looks at how long it may last.

headlines.title: Jack Draper beaten after controversial umpire call
headlines.description: Jack Draper is penalised with a controversial hindrance call as Daniil Medvedev ends the Briton's Indian Wells title defence in the quarter-finals.

headlines.title: Suspect dead after ramming vehicle into Michigan synagogue
headlines.description: The FBI says the incident is being investigated as a "targeted act of violence against the Jewish community".

headlines.title: Lost Doctor Who episodes found in 'eclectic' collection
headlines.description: The Doctor's assistant Peter Purves was invited to a screening of the episodes in Leicester.

headlines.title: Boy, 15, charged over stabbing of teenage girl at Norfolk school
headlines.description: Armed police were sent to the scene and pictured as they entered classrooms.

headlines.title: AI toys for young children need tighter rules, researchers warn
headlines.description: In first study of its kind, Cambridge researchers found AI toys could misread some children's emotions.

headlines.title: PM says Mandelson appointment was mistake as No 10 denies cover-up
headlines.description: Documents show Sir Keir Starmer was warned the peer's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein posed a "reputational risk".

headlines.title: Mission accomplished? The 2003 boast that haunts today's Iran conflict
headlines.description: The echoes between the conflicts are certainly there but there are also profound differences.

headlines.title: US eases Russia oil sanctions as Iran war pushes up energy prices
headlines.description: Washington says a 30-day waiver is only for sanctioned Russian oil already loaded on vessels at sea.

headlines.title: 'I missed my chemo and have a £12,000 hotel bill': British holidaymakers stranded by Iran war
headlines.description: Flights are restricted due to the conflict leaving people stuck running up bills for rooms and food.

headlines.title: Americans on Iran strikes: 'What if this turns into a forever war?'
headlines.description: Six US voters tell the BBC how they feel about the major US military intervention in Iran.

headlines.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

headlines.title: French vote tests polarised electorate with right hoping to win control of Paris
headlines.description: The highest-profile contest is for the mayorship of Paris - which has been under left-wing control for 25 years.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: This dog was Best in Show at Crufts - what other prize did he win?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: The Papers:  'Hidden hand of Putin' and 'This lying PM'
headlines.description: Putin accused of helping Iran and Tories say Starmer lied about Mandelson files - Friday's papers.

headlines.title: The US may move some of its anti-missile system - and it's sparking unease in South Korea
headlines.description: Reports say the US may be moving parts of its Thaad system to the Middle East, despite Seoul's opposition.

headlines.title: A young party led by a rapper wins a huge mandate - and Nepal steps into the unknown
headlines.description: Balen Shah's RSP has achieved a feat many thought impossible in Nepal: a sweeping majority.

headlines.title: King expressed 'concern' over Alberta separatists, say First Nations chiefs
headlines.description: Indigenous leaders made Charles aware of the "threat" posed by the movement during a meeting in London, they say.

headlines.title: MPs 'deeply troubled' by BBC World Service funding uncertainty
headlines.description: The current funding agreement between the BBC and the government expires at the end of this month.

headlines.title: Churchill's granddaughter happy with his picture being replaced on £5 note
headlines.description: Emma Soames rejected Nigel Farage's statement that the currency change was "wokery".

headlines.title: KPop Demon Hunters to return as Netflix announces sequel
headlines.description: The sequel brings back the co-directors of the first film, which was a smash-hit for the streaming service.

headlines.title: Man's blue skin A&E panic was just bed sheet dye
headlines.description: Tommy Lynch rushed to hospital when he woke with blue skin but it turned out to be from his sheets.

headlines.title: PwC boss says young recruits are hungry for careers
headlines.description: Last year the consultancy cut its graduate intake, but UK boss Marco Amitrano says it is still worth getting a degree.

headlines.title: Nepal election results - Balendra Shah's party wins in a landslide
headlines.description: This election is the first since a violent youth-led uprising toppled the government in 2025.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Taking Back Control: Why ‘Agency’ Could Be The Next Big Idea In Politics
headlines.description: A top pollster explains how a sense of powerlessness amongst voters is shaping politics.

headlines.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader (Sort Of) Speaks Out
headlines.description: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first message as Supreme Leader, but doesn’t appear in person.

headlines.title: Tobey Maguire stars as Spider-Man in the classic 2002 film
headlines.description: Peter Parker gains superpowers, but it takes a tragedy to teach him how to be a hero.

headlines.title: The numbers behind Borthwick's nightmare Six Nations
headlines.description: BBC Sport examines what has gone wrong with England's Six Nations campaign with their final game against France approaching.

headlines.title: Fastest to 100 wins - Europa specialist Emery sets Villa record
headlines.description: Unai Emery becomes the quickest of the three Aston Villa managers to reach 100 victories as his side defeat Lille in the Europa League last 16.

headlines.title: Draper beaten after controversial hindrance call
headlines.description: Jack Draper is penalised with a controversial hindrance call as Daniil Medvedev ends the Briton's Indian Wells title defence in the quarter-finals.

headlines.title: 'Everyone at Man Utd loves him' - Owen backs Carrick for job
headlines.description: Michael Owen says he "can't believe people are questioning" whether Michael Carrick should become the permanent manager of Manchester United.

headlines.title: Clayton overcomes gout to crush Humphries in final
headlines.description: Jonny Clayton struggles with gout but still wins night six of the Premier League in Nottingham by crushing Luke Humphries 6-1 in the final.

headlines.title: Sutton's predictions v YouTuber Angry Ginge
headlines.description: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on YouTuber Angry Ginge plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.
